x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Eligibility Evaluation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The process that verifies whether a financial product qualifies for rollover based on predefined rules and conditions.

Comment:
Examples

Loan repayments must be up to date

Deposit must reach maturity

Customer must meet credit requirements

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Automatic Renewal
x-enumeration-literals.description: Extends the maturity or term of a deposit, investment, or loan without requiring manual intervention from the customer.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Notification
x-enumeration-literals.description:
May notify customers about the rollover or request confirmation based on product or regulatory requirements.

Notifications may include
Maturity reminders
Rollover confirmation
Updated interest rate notification

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Adjustment Management
x-enumeration-literals.description: Rate / Terms Adjustment: Applies current interest rates, fees, or terms for the new period if applicable.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Monitoring and Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The function that tracks and records rollover activities for operational oversight.

Includes
Rollover history
Transaction records
Performance tracking

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Reporting
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The generation of operational and financial reports related to rollover activities.

Comment:
Examples
Total rollovers executed
Rollover value reports
Loan restructuring reports

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rollover Tracking & Reporting
x-enumeration-literals.description: Maintains records of rollover events, new maturity dates, and updated terms for auditing and customer statements.
